description:
You are the release-manager for the “Acme-Payments” platform.  
    A new deployment introduces three micro-services that need to be reachable
    from outside the cluster.  Because you do **not** have super-user privileges inside
    this sandbox, we keep a *simulated* copy of the system firewall rules under

        /home/user/firewall/base.rules          (already present, **read-only**)
        /home/user/firewall/                    (writable by you)

    Your job is to:
        1. Generate an updated ruleset called
              /home/user/firewall/current.rules
           by copying the existing base file and then inserting **exactly** three new
           ACCEPT rules, one per micro-service, **directly above the final “COMMIT” line**.
           The services and ports are:

               microservice-auth     → 8081/tcp
               microservice-data     → 8082/tcp
               microservice-gateway  → 8443/tcp

           Each rule must be preceded by a comment line that starts with a hash (#)
           containing the service name exactly as shown above.

        2. Produce a deployment log file at
              /home/user/release_logs/firewall_update_20231020.log
           The directory already exists and is writable.
           The log **must** have the following five lines, in this
           exact order (newline-terminated, no extra spaces):

               START:2023-10-20T12:00:00Z
               ALLOW:microservice-auth:8081
               ALLOW:microservice-data:8082
               ALLOW:microservice-gateway:8443
               END:SUCCESS

           • The automated tests are case-sensitive.  
           • No additional lines or trailing spaces are permitted.

    Verification details that tests will check:
        • /home/user/firewall/current.rules exists and is world-readable.
        • It is identical to /home/user/firewall/base.rules **except** for the
          three added comment+rule pairs inserted immediately before the original
          COMMIT.
        • /home/user/release_logs/firewall_update_20231020.log exists,
          contains exactly the 5 lines shown above, and is world-readable.

    Important constraints:
        • Do not modify /home/user/firewall/base.rules.
        • Do not remove or reorder any pre-existing lines in the ruleset.
        • Use only regular user privileges.
        • Feel free to create helper scripts inside /home/user/firewall but
          clean up any temporary files you create.

    Once finished, double-check that both the rules file and the log file match
    the required formats, because the grader will do a byte-for-byte comparison.
truth:
<!--  PRE-EXISTING STATE  -->
    File /home/user/firewall/base.rules is present at task start with the
    following exact bytes (newline = LF):

*filter
:INPUT DROP [0:0]
:FORWARD DROP [0:0]
:OUTPUT ACCEPT [0:0]
-A INPUT -m conntrack --ctstate ESTABLISHED,RELATED -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -i lo -j ACCEPT
COMMIT

    Directory /home/user/release_logs already exists and is writable.

    <!--  EXPECTED FINAL STATE  -->
    1) /home/user/firewall/current.rules must contain:

*filter
:INPUT DROP [0:0]
:FORWARD DROP [0:0]
:OUTPUT ACCEPT [0:0]
-A INPUT -m conntrack --ctstate ESTABLISHED,RELATED -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -i lo -j ACCEPT
# microservice-auth
-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 8081 -j ACCEPT
# microservice-data
-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 8082 -j ACCEPT
# microservice-gateway
-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 8443 -j ACCEPT
COMMIT

      Permissions: 0644 or more restrictive, owned by user.

    2) /home/user/release_logs/firewall_update_20231020.log must contain exactly:

START:2023-10-20T12:00:00Z
ALLOW:microservice-auth:8081
ALLOW:microservice-data:8082
ALLOW:microservice-gateway:8443
END:SUCCESS

      (Five lines, each ending with LF, no carriage returns, no trailing spaces.)
      Permissions: 0644 or more restrictive, owned by user.

    The grader will:
        • diff /home/user/firewall/current.rules against the string above;
          any deviation fails.
        • diff /home/user/release_logs/firewall_update_20231020.log against
          the string above; any deviation fails.
